moduleTitle: Materials, Processes, Test Variables, and Recording Media

lessons.title: Materials and Manufacturing Processes for Visual Testing
objectives: Use diagrams and drawings to plan and execute a visual test
Describe raw materials and primary process materials from ingot through plate, forging, casting, tubing, extrusion, and wire
Identify joining processes and joint configurations that visual testing must address

lessons.title: Fabricated Components, In-Service Materials, Coatings, and Other Applications
objectives: Apply VT techniques to fabricated components including pressure vessels, pumps, valves, fasteners, and piping systems
Recognize in-service degradation including wear and corrosion/erosion, and the use of microscopy for in-service evaluation
Describe VT of coatings and other applications such as ceramics, composites, plastics, and bearings, and relate codes and personnel qualification requirements to the work

lessons.title: Test Variables Affecting Visual Test Results
objectives: Explain how equipment, material, and discontinuity variables affect visual test results
Determine indication dimensions and apply sampling/scanning procedure controls
Describe how personnel (human factors) variables influence detection and interpretation
cp105Topics: 4.1 Equipment variables affecting test results, including type and intensity of light
4.2 Material variables affecting test results, including variations of surface finish
4.5 Sampling/scanning procedure variables affecting test results
4.7 Personnel (human factors) variables affecting test results

lessons.title: Recording Media, Photography Applications, and Personnel Certification
objectives: Apply photography to specific VT applications including welds, pressure vessels, pumps, valves, castings, forgings, and microcircuits
Identify code requirements (AWS, ASME, etc.) for audio/video records and use electronic and magnetic media for VT documentation
Summarize personnel qualification and certification requirements related to recording and reporting
